description: For did:key, signature.key_id MUST have the form did:key:z<mb>#z<mb> with the fragment byte-equal to the DID's method-specific identifier (W3C did:key convention; RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11.1 step 1). A key_id whose fragment names a DIFFERENT key than the DID part MUST be rejected with key_resolution_failed — there is no DID document in which a second key could live, so a foreign fragment is structurally meaningless and suggests a copy-paste of did:web key-management habits or an attempted confusion attack.

input.scenario: Publish where the key_id fragment is a valid-looking multibase key that differs from the DID's key part
input.note: DID part is the sig-003 test key; fragment is the sig-001 test key's multibase — both individually valid encodings.

expected.behavior: Step 1 of §5.11.1: fragment ≠ method-specific identifier → key_resolution_failed (permanent). The resolver MUST compare byte-for-byte and MUST NOT resolve 'whichever of the two keys verifies'. A missing fragment fails the same step with the same code (mirroring the did:web missing-fragment rule, RFC-ACDP-0001 §5.11 step 1). Note the agent_id ↔ key_id DID-portion binding is a separate, earlier check that returns key_not_authorized on mismatch — unchanged from did:web.
